title: Закон за достъп до обществена информация (ЗДОИ)
description: Bulgaria's freedom of information legislation, implementing Directive 2003/98/EC on the re-use of public sector information. Establishes the right of access to public information held by state bodies and the conditions for re-use of public sector information.

provisions.title: Subject and scope
provisions.content: This Act regulates the public relations relating to the right of access to public information and the re-use of public sector information. This Act also implements the requirements of Directive 2003/98/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the re-use of public sector information and Directive (EU) 2019/1024 on open data and the re-use of public sector information.

provisions.title: Public information defined
provisions.content: Public information within the meaning of this Act shall be any information related to public life in the Republic of Bulgaria that gives a citizen the opportunity to form his or her own opinion regarding the activity of the bodies obliged under this Act. Public information shall be the information created or held by state bodies, their territorial units, and bodies of local self-government.

provisions.title: Types of public information
provisions.content: Public information shall be official and administrative. Official information is information contained in acts of state bodies and of bodies of local self-government issued in the exercise of their powers. Administrative information is information collected, created and kept in connection with the official information, as well as on the occasion of the activity of the bodies and their administrations.

provisions.title: Right holders
provisions.content: Every citizen of the Republic of Bulgaria shall have the right of access to public information under the conditions and procedure established by this Act, unless another law provides for a special procedure for seeking, obtaining and disseminating such information. Foreigners and stateless persons shall also have the right of access to public information under this Act. Legal persons shall also have the right of access to public information.

provisions.title: Obliged bodies
provisions.content: Obliged bodies under this Act are: state bodies; bodies of local self-government; public-law entities; entities financed from the consolidated state budget; mass media financed from the state or municipal budget; natural or legal persons only regarding the public information which they have been obliged to create or hold by virtue of law.

provisions.title: Restrictions on access
provisions.content: The right of access to public information may not be exercised where the information: constitutes a state secret or other legally protected secret; affects the interests of third parties and their explicit written consent has not been obtained, unless there is an overriding public interest in disclosure; access is restricted by virtue of another law.

provisions.title: Request for access
provisions.content: Access to public information shall be provided upon a written request or an oral request. The request shall contain: the full name of the applicant or, in the case of a legal person, its name and registered office; a description of the information sought; the preferred form of access to the information. No reasons need be stated for requesting access to public information.

provisions.title: Form of access
provisions.content: Access to public information shall be provided in one or more of the following forms: review of the information at the premises of the body; a paper copy; a copy on electronic medium; oral information. The form of access requested by the applicant shall be provided unless: there are no technical means to provide the requested form; the requested form is unreasonably costly for the body.

provisions.title: Time limits
provisions.content: The obliged body shall issue a decision granting or refusing access to public information within 14 days of receiving the request. The time limit may be extended by up to 10 days where the request requires a large amount of information or where the body needs to seek the consent of a third party. The body shall notify the applicant of the extension within the original time limit.

provisions.title: Decision on access
provisions.content: The decision granting access to public information shall specify: the extent to which access is provided to the information requested; the period during which access is provided; the place at which access will be provided; the form of access; the amount of the costs for providing access. The decision refusing access shall be motivated and shall state the legal and factual grounds for the refusal.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4 - Re-use of Public Sector Information
provisions.title: Right of re-use
provisions.content: Any person shall have the right to re-use public sector information made available by the obliged bodies. The re-use of information shall be free of charge or at marginal cost unless otherwise provided by law. The obliged bodies shall make public sector information available in open, machine-readable formats and through application programming interfaces (APIs) where technically feasible.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4 - Re-use of Public Sector Information
provisions.title: Conditions for re-use
provisions.content: The obliged bodies may impose conditions on the re-use of information, which shall be objective, proportionate, non-discriminatory and justified by a public interest objective. Conditions shall not unnecessarily restrict possibilities for re-use and shall not be used to restrict competition. Standard licences for re-use shall be made available in digital format and shall be capable of being processed electronically.

provisions.title: Proactive publication
provisions.content: Each obliged body shall publish on its internet site: a description of its powers, organisational structure, and functions; the acts adopted in the exercise of its powers; the name, address, telephone number and e-mail of the official designated to receive requests for access; the reports on received requests and decisions issued; datasets in open format where available.

provisions.title: Right of appeal
provisions.content: Decisions to refuse access to public information, as well as the explicit or tacit refusal to issue a decision, may be appealed before the administrative courts under the procedure of the Administrative Procedure Code. The appeal shall be filed within 14 days of the notification of the decision or, in the case of tacit refusal, within 14 days of the expiry of the time limit for issuing a decision.

provisions.title: Sanctions
provisions.content: An official who fails to comply with a decision granting access to public information shall be subject to a fine of BGN 200 to BGN 2,000 unless the act constitutes a criminal offence. An official who, without lawful grounds, fails to issue a decision on a request for access within the time limit shall be subject to a fine of BGN 100 to BGN 500. For repeated violations, the fine shall be doubled.

definitions.definition: Any information related to public life in the Republic of Bulgaria that gives a citizen the opportunity to form his or her own opinion regarding the activity of the bodies obliged under this Act.
Information contained in acts of state bodies and of bodies of local self-government issued in the exercise of their powers.
